what_it_does: Defines a single density knob (--density) and a set of density-aware spacing tokens (--d-1..--d-12) computed as calc(base * var(--density)). Provides density-scaled control/row heights (with a touch-size floor), and three data-density presets (compact 0.8, comfortable 1, spacious 1.25) that also retune fluid spacing and the gutter.
observations: --density default is 1 (comfortable); the --d-* scale derives 4/8/12/16/20/24/32/48px each multiplied by --density
--control-h uses max(var(--touch-min), calc(40px*--density)) so density never shrinks controls below the touch floor
compact and spacious presets additionally override --space-fluid-md (clamp) and --gutter (var(--s-4) vs var(--s-12))
Inline @kind annotations (other / spacing) tag each declaration, presumably for tooling/the token registry
Header notes the fixed 8px --s-* scale stays for hard structural measures while --d-* are for new components
standouts: A single multiplier knob re-tunes the whole layout's breathing room per surface, explicitly framed as the spacing analogue of --zone-intensity for colour
The touch-min floor on control height prevents density compaction from violating tappable minimums
conventions: Follows the knob-pattern (a data-* attribute driving a CSS var) used across the system
Two parallel spacing scales by intent: fixed --s-* for structure, scalable --d-* for components
Uses @kind inline comment annotations consistent with a token-tagging convention
notes: One --density multiplier scales all component spacing (--d-*) and control sizing per surface via data-density, with a touch-size floor protecting controls; structural --s-* stays fixed.
questions: Where are --touch-min, --s-4, --s-12, and --space-fluid-md defined that this file references?
What consumes the @kind annotations (a token registry / emitter)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header describes one density knob scaling breathing room with presets and a touch floor, and the file implements exactly that.
apparent_themes: single knob driving many derived values
per-surface adaptivity (data-dense desktop vs roomy mobile)
accessibility floor (touch minimum preserved under compaction)
separation of structural vs component spacing
intention_signals: Aims to let one attribute re-tune an entire UI's spacing for context (dense data views vs comfortable mobile) by multiplying a coherent spacing scale, while keeping hard structural measures and tappable minimums protected.
novelty: Casting spacing as a continuous density multiplier analogous to a colour-intensity knob, with derived spacing tokens new components reach for instead of fixed pixels.
contribution: Contributes the density axis: the --density knob, the density-aware --d-* spacing scale, density-scaled control/row sizing with a touch floor, and the compact/comfortable/spacious presets including fluid-spacing and gutter overrides.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS file defining a --density multiplier and density-aware spacing tokens (--d-*), density-scaled control/row heights with a touch floor, and compact/comfortable/spacious data-density presets that also retune fluid spacing and gutter.
intention_for_embedding: To let a single per-surface attribute scale a UI's breathing room continuously, treating spacing density as a knob like colour intensity, while protecting structural measures and touch-size minimums.
